text.title: VUI Error Recovery Ladder
text.summary: Error taxonomy, three-rung re-prompt ladder rules, escalation triggers, and copy guidelines for voice interface error handling.

section.title: Error Taxonomy
section.p: Four error types in voice interfaces, each requiring a distinct recovery approach:

list.item: No-input: user is silent. Cause: user did not understand they should speak, distracted, or endpointing cut off early. Recovery: brief re-ask with response-timing hint.
No-match: speech recognized but intent not matched. Cause: out-of-vocabulary utterance, accent mismatch, NLU gap. Recovery: re-ask with example phrases from training data.
Ambiguous: multiple valid intent interpretations with similar confidence. Recovery: ask a disambiguating clarifying question — do not guess.
System error: technical failure (API timeout, platform error). Recovery: apologize, state the problem briefly, suggest retry or alternative channel.

rule.statement: Structure every re-prompt sequence as exactly three rungs: rung 1 (brief re-ask), rung 2 (constructive re-ask with two example phrases), rung 3 (escalation to visual fallback, DTMF, or human agent). Cap at three rungs — escalate on the fourth attempt, never loop.
rule.rationale: Users who hear the same phrase three times in a row perceive the system as broken. Three rungs balance error recovery opportunity against abandonment risk. Looping past rung 3 has near-zero recovery rate and high frustration cost.

example.code:
Rung 1 (no-match): "I didn't catch that. What's the city?"
Rung 2 (constructive): "I'm having trouble understanding. Try saying something like 'New York' or 'Chicago'."
Rung 3 (escalation): "Let me try a different approach. You can press 1 to continue with our menu, or say 'agent' to speak with someone."
example.note: Rung 2 uses exactly two example phrases from NLU training data. Rung 3 offers both DTMF and voice escalation paths.

rule.statement: Vary the rung-2 example phrases per session. Rotate across 3–5 phrase sets so users who retry the skill do not learn to mimic one phrase, masking the true NLU coverage gap.
rule.rationale: Users who successfully mimic a single example phrase suppress the signal that the NLU training data is too narrow. Rotating phrases reveals the true no-match surface area.

section.title: Copy Rules

rule.statement: Never use blame language: ban "you said wrong", "invalid", "didn't work", "I'm sorry" stacking (more than once per session). Voice the system's limitation, not the user's failure.
rule.rationale: Blame language causes immediate abandonment. Users who feel blamed do not retry — they leave and write negative reviews. "I didn't catch that" attributes the failure to the system; "You said something I couldn't understand" attributes it to the user.

rule.statement: For telephony channels, always offer DTMF fallback ("press 1 for...") on rung 3. Never escalate to human-agent-only without a self-service option.
rule.rationale: Telephony channels add 8 kHz codec loss and cross-talk that no copy fix mitigates. DTMF remains reliable when ASR fails. Human-agent-only escalation has queue costs; self-service DTMF is free.

section.reference: CI lint: flag missing rung 3 escalation, missing rung 2 examples, banned blame phrases
